reviewBasis: Newest match first; recordings inside a match are sorted by length.

mainFeedback.title: Samira: stop giving wins back
mainFeedback.focus: Honest read: your biggest leak is not damage. It is refusing to cash out: staying at lethal HP, re-fighting after winning, and chasing sideways when structure is already open.
mainFeedback.rule: If the next action does not create HP safety, wave crash, turret, or nexus pressure, do not take it.
mainFeedback.nextRep: Next game: cash out before the second fight.
mainFeedback.whyTrust: The clips show the same leak in three forms: lethal-HP lane stay, low-health re-fight, and open-base drift. That is specific enough to practice immediately.
mainFeedback.pattern: You already find fights. The coachable problem is making the second decision boring enough to actually win: reset, crash, hit tower, hit nexus, then fight again only if it protects that payout.
checklist: First win becomes wave or structure.
Open base means no sideways chase.
mainFeedback.reviewLimit: Replay review is based on sampled frames and visible state, not raw inputs or full cooldown telemetry.

detectedChampions.name: Samira
detectedChampions.evidence: Manual storyboard review of the May 18 full recording: early lane death at lethal HP, later kills and turret/base conversion after grouping.
detectedChampions.improvement: Before committing, know what the win buys: crash, plate, tower, dragon move, recall, or end.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: Major Structure Conversion Miss — Chasing Instead of Nexus Push
recordings.feedbackTitle: Major Structure Conversion Miss — Chasing Instead of Nexus Push
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You chased remaining enemies deep into base instead of immediately turning to hit open inhibitor and Nexus towers after winning teamfights. Fix: After acing or driving away the enemy team in their base, stop chasing kills and immediately focus on hitting open inhibitors or Nexus to close the game.
recordings.gameDetail: This created extra risk of throw and lost the window to end while the enemy was dead. The big lesson is: always turn for base towers when the enemy team is down or fleeing in their base.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:34 Player uses ability on minions in lane near allied tower; 6:52 Player is dead and has shop open after first turret falls; 8:01 Champion respawns at fountain and begins walking out
recordings.goodThing: Showed strong confidence when fighting grouped with teammates, converting multi-kills and big value from ult.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira wins games by ending quickly when her reset power is highest, and these missed tower hits are the main thing holding back closing winnable games fast.
recordings.evidence: Multiple dead enemies and open structures are visible on the minimap while Samira moves past instead of attacking them, especially after large teamfight wins.
recordings.pattern: After successful fights, Samira looks for enemy champions outside the base, ignoring exposed inhibitors and Nexus towers; this delays the win and keeps the game risky.
recordings.diamondRule: When you win a fight in the enemy base and enemies are dead or backing, always hit inhibitor/Nexus towers immediately, not champions.
recordings.drill: In your next three games, after any fight win in the enemy base, ping towers and auto-attack the nearest open structure until it’s down or you’re forced away.

nuance: Samira accumulated large shutdown gold; if a throw happened near enemy spawns, risk would flip the game.
Wave states mid and top open opportunities for double inhib/delete Nexus, but decision-making defaulted to chasing.
Confidence with combos looks strong, but macro focus is lost after the fight ends, especially around open base.
Vision isn’t the problem; hesitation is between targeting objectives vs. hunting respawning opponents.
Recall was delayed even with full inventory on a few occasions, showing a tendency to overstay after aces.
recordings.reviewLimit: The timeline is sparse before 16 minutes, so early reset or wave control discipline can’t be graded here, but the late-game structure choice is provably off.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: Delayed Reset After Major Fight
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You delayed your recall and stayed on map with high gold after fights and major objectives, missing the window to convert your lead into items. Fix: Immediately reset and spend gold after a big teamfight, major kills, or objective, especially when you are safe and your team has tempo.
recordings.gameDetail: Alan built a strong early lead with multiple kills and coordinated fights (notably bot and mid), then continued to farm and pressure without resetting after major events. This habit wastes teamfight wins and delays your item spike, making future engages riskier than necessary. Use every window after a major win or objective to convert for immediate power, not just map presence.
recordings.eventEvidence: 14:32 Post-fight window after a kill, holding gold without recalling
recordings.goodThing: You chained aggressive plays and picked correct fights with good follow-up on support engages.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira's power spikes hard with items, and holding excess gold delays your snowball—using your lead directly increases your kill and push threat while minimizing risk.
recordings.evidence: 14:32 Post-fight window after a kill, holding gold without recalling
recordings.pattern: After every successful fight or major objective, you farm or push another wave with thousands of gold instead of immediately resetting, which creates deadtime for power spikes and team momentum.
recordings.diamondRule: No matter how fed you are, always recall and spend after a key fight or secured objective if you can do it safely—snowballing is only real when you convert to items.
recordings.drill: In your next three games, immediately recall after your team gets 2+ kills or a major objective, check your gold vs item breakpoint, and buy before rejoining fights.

clockAnchors.description: Champion running towards bottom lane past outer turret
Samira chases enemies in bot lane, both at low HP
Samira and ally attacking enemies under mid turret
Samira kills an enemy near top side outer turret

clockMoments.description: Post-fight window after a kill, holding gold without recalling

nuance: You consistently farmed and applied pressure when strong, but held large unspent gold amounts past multiple teamfight wins.
After winning fights or taking objectives, you immediately looked for further fights or farm without evaluating your recall window.
Cross-map movements (after winning bot, then moving towards dragon and mid) happened with unspent gold, increasing risk and lowering kill conversion.
You responded correctly to support engages and often targeted the right champion in skirmishes.
You sometimes looked for structure pressure, but did not reset even after the enemy was dead and map was won.
recordings.reviewLimit: Frames do not show the exact ult/cooldown management or missed kill windows during fights, but gold and map reset discipline is fully visible and provable.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: Stop Splitting from the Winning Push
recordings.feedbackTitle: Stop Splitting from the Winning Push
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You repeatedly left open inhibitor/nexus turrets to hit side lane waves or camps instead of ending the game when enemy base was exposed. Fix: When your team has super minions and can threaten the nexus or inhib turrets, always group and commit to the final push instead of splitting or clearing jungle—only reset after a failed engage or if all threats are dead.
recordings.gameDetail: These were windows to end the game, but splitting wasted pressure, allowing enemy respawns. Even with high kill numbers and fed status, the disconnect between fights and structure focus slowed your win. The lesson: when you break into the base with numbers, convert directly on exposed structures—delay or reset only if there’s real risk.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:32 Samira is fighting near bot lane river with another champion close to a wall.; 5:31 Samira and ally are pushing bot lane near enemy outer turret amidst enemy minions.; 12:12 Samira moving in river near top lane, approaching scuttle crab.
recordings.goodThing: You snowballed early fights into high gold and routinely showed up for team fights when grouped.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira’s kit is strongest grouped with teammates in base pushes where you can chain engage and ult off allied CC; this recording repeatedly showed map pressure being lost when you solo split instead of ending.
recordings.evidence: Exposure of inhibitor and nexus turrets on minimap while Samira is shown away from the push, plus super minion pressure unaccompanied.
recordings.pattern: You consistently rotated away to side lanes or jungle right after breaking open the enemy base or getting big teamfight wins, leaving your team without max damage on critical structures and giving the enemy time to respawn.
recordings.diamondRule: If inhibitor or nexus turrets are exposed and your team can group, always commit to the base push first—side lane waves and jungle camps matter after the threat is removed.
recordings.drill: In your next game, after the first open inhibitor or nexus turret, consciously direct movements toward grouping mid/base for the finish and do not hit a side camp or wave until forced to reset.

nuance: Samira had heavy early snowball and plenty of gold, putting her far ahead.
Team consistently had open access to inhibitor/nexus turrets off won fights.
Samira left base pushes multiple times for camps or outlying waves with no threat on map.
Extending splits invited stalled endings and allowed more enemy respawns.
Grouping for structure finish is mechanically easy and gives clear win payoff with fed Samira.
recordings.reviewLimit: Sampled frames cannot show precise enemy death timers or every macro call, and cannot prove full map state between frames, but the visible map pressure and turret exposure are clear enough for this repeatable pattern.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: Push for towers after bot win
recordings.feedbackTitle: Push for towers after bot win
recordings.feedback: Mistake: After finding successful botlane fights, you repeatedly chase into jungle/fog or linger near lane instead of immediately using your wave for turret plates and first tower. Fix: After a botlane win, focus on crashing the wave and pressuring the outer turret until your team is forced away, only leaving if the enemy spawns or you have to reset for HP/mana.
recordings.gameDetail: There were also moments when you split off from minions or left the lane before turret fell. The simple lesson: always cash in fights with a tower push when possible, especially as Samira with wave clear.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:45 Post-fight with allies alive, but Samira lingers near bot lane with wave instead of pressuring low turret for plates.; 7:19 After another bot win, Samira and Lux in lane but not immediately pushing for tower while enemy team retreats, missing structure opportunity.
recordings.goodThing: You consistently followed up support engages in bot lane and snowballed with strong early fight impact.
recordings.whyTrust: Towers are the biggest source of reliable team gold after a won fight, and Samira's fast clear lets you snowball leads quickly with plates if you prioritize them after kills.
recordings.evidence: Bot wave is at enemy turret and you're alive with teammates after a double kill, yet turret pressure is delayed or missed.
recordings.pattern: After winning 2v2s or 3v3s, you move toward river/tribush or hesitate instead of always hitting turret when it's safe and the wave is ready; this delays or loses early plate income.
recordings.diamondRule: Always push for plates or turret after a successful bot fight unless you see multiple respawn timers ending or lack the HP to stay.
recordings.drill: In your next three bot wins, stay for plates or the tower until either the enemy arrives or you must base, no exceptions.

clockAnchors.description: Samira runs down bot lane with allied minions; all ally respawn timers visible.
Samira and Lux in lane, enemy minions present; two enemy champions retreating.

clockMoments.description: Post-fight with allies alive, but Samira lingers near bot lane with wave instead of pressuring low turret for plates.
After another bot win, Samira and Lux in lane but not immediately pushing for tower while enemy team retreats, missing structure opportunity.

nuance: Fight mechanics are solid and you convert early kills, but leave turret windows open.
Waves are sometimes up at enemy bot tower post-fight with no enemies alive, but you don't always capitalize.
You rarely overstay for plates versus full respawn, but often leave before the turret is taken or guaranteed safe recall.
Later roam timing generally improves, but the habit of not instinctively pushing tower after bot win remains.
Samira's passive lets her quickly clear waves for plates, but the gold is missed if you disengage too soon.
recordings.reviewLimit: Frame density is enough to confirm the pattern around bot structure conversion, but not enough to evaluate all recall choices or judge mid/late split pushes.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: Reset Discipline After Inhibitor Push
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You overstayed aggressively after breaking inhibitor and winning a fight, pushing into a full respawned enemy team instead of resetting with your gold lead. Fix: After taking the inhibitor or a clean tower win, immediately back off and reset when the enemy team is about to respawn, especially with shutdown bounties, rather than forcing a bad 5v5 in their base.
recordings.gameDetail: The main lesson: convert lead to resets and objectives, not risky fountain base fights.
recordings.eventEvidence: 16:31 Post-inhibitor destruction; team should reset here, but stays in base.; 17:17 Caught overstaying in enemy base, 3 teammates already dead instead of recalling after inhib.; 18:31 Re-engaging down mid after respawn instead of reset, ignoring chance to regroup.
recordings.goodThing: You secured multiple clean kills and successfully took down mid lane objectives when your team had the numbers advantage.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira loses all fight footing when the enemy team respawns together in base, and every time you push past an inhibitor against five, you throw your shutdown gold lead—this pattern is in multiple end-game teamfights in this footage.
recordings.evidence: 16:31 Post-inhibitor destruction; team should reset here, but stays in base.; 17:17 Caught overstaying in enemy base, 3 teammates already dead instead of recalling after inhib.; 18:31 Re-engaging down mid after respawn instead of reset, ignoring chance to regroup.
recordings.pattern: Repeatedly, after breaking structures with a numbers/tempo advantage, you extend the play instead of recalling, giving the enemy a coordinated respawn defense and losing shutdown gold.
recordings.diamondRule: When you break an inhibitor and the enemy team respawns, always reset and spend your gold; never force base fights with shutdowns up.
recordings.drill: In your next game, after taking a mid inhibitor or an inner tower, immediately recall and ping your team back—track how many fights you avoid versus the respawn timer.

nuance: Pushed up mid after winning teamfight, took inhib with no minions left for further pressure.
Enemy team respawned as fight was forced deep in base, losing advantage.
Multiple allied deaths visible post-inhibitor, with strong gold bounties handed back.
Samira keeps fighting and chasing for kills after taking key objectives instead of spending over 2k gold shown.
Missed safe reset window even when teammates began to die and enemies were stacking in spawn.
recordings.reviewLimit: Frames do not show the full post-wipe map state or objective timers, but the repeated inhibitor push-overstay and shutdown feed are clear.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: Lingered with gold, low HP, and no reset discipline
recordings.feedbackTitle: Lingered with gold, low HP, and no reset discipline
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed in lane and kept pushing and fighting with less than half HP and over 1500 gold, risking shutdowns and dying to enemy pressure. Fix: Immediately shove the wave after a kill or force and reset when your HP is low and you have over 1300 gold, especially if enemies can return or collapse.
recordings.gameDetail: After an early skirmish under your own tower, you push back for kills but stay on the map at low HP, circling from wave to wave with nearly 2000 gold while the enemy bot lane respawns and returns. Later in the clip you continue to farm and hit tower, exposing your shutdown and making yourself a free target for respawns or jungle collapse. The core lesson: after big fights or forced retreats, shove the wave then reset immediately before you get punished.
recordings.eventEvidence: The visible pattern is staying in lane with low HP and a large gold stack instead of turning the skirmish into a shove/reset.
recordings.goodThing: You capitalized on enemy missteps for multiple low-HP outplays around 3:26 and 4:23.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira is extremely vulnerable to return kills and shutdowns after skirmishes; resetting with a lead is the only way to turn early power into a snowball and not throw advantage.
recordings.evidence: Low HP and high gold after fights with full waves visible, no recall even as enemies respawn and approach.
recordings.pattern: After favorable trades and even after forced enemy recalls, you stayed in lane with kill bounty and no HP, risking throw for extra CS or plate gold instead of cashing in lead.
recordings.diamondRule: Always reset after a big fight or skirmish when you have over 1300 gold or are below 40% HP, unless you have full vision of all enemies.
recordings.drill: Next game, after any botlane fight or forced enemy recall, immediately shove the next wave and recall if your HP is under 60% or you have over 1300 gold—do not stay for extra waves.
timeline: Both you and your support low HP, enemy support returns, but you stay for next wave.
You secure another kill but still do not recall.
Continue clearing wave, still not recalling with high gold and low HP.
Recall finally begins after multiple greedy waves.
Stay hitting under tower even with enemy respawns in vision.

clockAnchors.description: Player is moving alone in bottom lane; minions have spawned.
Player is clearing a wave in bottom lane as dragon is near.

clockMoments.description: Staying on the map to clear a wave with low HP and high gold after prior fights, risking shutdown with no reset.

nuance: You consistently took aggressive trades but failed to disengage after advantageous outcomes.
You gathered two kills and assists but greedily stayed under low HP for farm.
There was ample gold for a full buy, yet you delayed recall even as enemies re-entered lane.
Clear minimap indicators showed potential jungle or mid collapse with no vision coverage.
You risked shutdown gold and lane priority by greedy extra waves rather than using your lead to pressure reset timing.
recordings.reviewLimit: The frames do not show any actual deaths during this period, so it's possible you escaped punishment this time, but the reset error is clear and repeatable for harder games.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: Finish-window all-in
recordings.feedback: Mistake: over-learning this clip would make you throw; the same dash is bad if it starts without wave, teammate body, or nexus pressure. Fix: full send only when every reset moves toward the payout.
recordings.gameDetail: At 14:21 the fight is already in their base, not in river or a side lane: allies and minions are in front, enemy defenders are packed near nexus, and Samira is close enough to chain resets without walking into fog. Learn this exactly: the all-in is good when the map is already ending; outside that condition, cash out first.
recordings.eventEvidence: 14:21 Engagement begins with allies, enemies, and minions in front of the nexus—fight is inside endgame window.; 14:33 Triple kill shows Samira chaining resets as the brawl stays next to the base, highlighting full send at finish timing.; 14:41 Penta kill lands as fight never leaves nexus area, confirming this full commit works since every reset advances the win.
recordings.goodThing: The strong part is the finish discipline: once the enemy line collapses, Samira stays with the ally/minion push and cleans through the defenders instead of drifting sideways.
recordings.whyTrust: The penta is not random: the clip shows the conditions that made it valid, with allies and minions already in base and every reset moving toward nexus.
recordings.evidence: Manual storyboard review of the May 20 highlight: visible base fight from 14:21 to 14:41, double/triple/quadra/penta sequence, and enemy base already open.
recordings.pattern: Samira's best-looking fight here works because the map already gives the exit and the payout. The dangerous habit would be copying the same depth when the fight is not already a base finish.
recordings.diamondRule: All-in only when the reset path and the payout are the same direction.
recordings.drill: Before E/R, ask: does every reset move me toward nexus, tower, dragon, Baron, or safety?
timeline: 14:21 - Samira is already inside the enemy base with several defenders alive and allied pressure in front.
14:33 - The reset chain reaches triple while the wave and allies still hold the base area.
14:41 - The penta lands with the enemy base open, which is the correct payoff condition.

clockAnchors.description: Several champions fighting near destroyed inhibitor, health bars visible.
Triple kill notification shown, champions fighting among minions in base.
Pentakill notification shown, surviving blue champions moving away from fight.

clockMoments.description: Engagement begins with allies, enemies, and minions in front of the nexus—fight is inside endgame window.
Triple kill shows Samira chaining resets as the brawl stays next to the base, highlighting full send at finish timing.
Penta kill lands as fight never leaves nexus area, confirming this full commit works since every reset advances the win.

nuance: The clip starts after the decision to enter, so it cannot prove whether the first click was disciplined.
The visible reason the play works is base geometry plus allies and minions, not just raw damage.
The penta is good evidence that the cleanup mechanics are there.
The next improvement is knowing when not to copy this same depth outside a finish window.
recordings.reviewLimit: The Riot highlight is only about 22 seconds, so the review claims the visible base fight and does not invent the earlier setup.

recordings.gameTypeSource: LeagueClient log
recordings.title: You drift after base opens
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you chase sideways after the base is already open. Fix: stand behind the first body in and hit the structure as soon as space is made.
recordings.gameDetail: Honest read: the useful footage starts after a desktop-capture section, and the lesson is still clear. Around 13:05 the map slows down: Mordekaiser walks away mid, an ally is channeling recall, and Samira is full HP with the lane already open, which is exactly the moment to choose wave/base route before another target appears.
recordings.eventEvidence: 13:05 Pause in action after base is opened, choice to hit structure or reset; 14:23 Team correctly grouping and hitting Nexus for structure conversion
recordings.goodThing: You did group back toward the base and finish the game instead of letting the push fully dissolve.
recordings.whyTrust: The win came from returning to the open base, not from a cleaner combo. Your damage was not the limiting factor; target discipline was.
recordings.evidence: 13:05 Pause in action after base is opened, choice to hit structure or reset; 14:23 Team correctly grouping and hitting Nexus for structure conversion
recordings.pattern: The winning pressure was already available. The mistake is treating a nearby champion like the win condition after the actual win condition is already open.
recordings.diamondRule: If a base structure is available, a chase is wrong unless it directly removes the body blocking the structure.
recordings.drill: Next game: when inhibitor or nexus is open, say front line, structure, no chase.

Team attacking the enemy Nexus after a kill

clockMoments.description: Pause in action after base is opened, choice to hit structure or reset

nuance: The most useful footage is the late-game base decision, not the scoreboard line.
The mistake is drifting after the base route is already available.
Samira's cleanest role here is finisher behind the first body, not fight starter.
The recording still contains desktop-capture segments from before the window-only recorder fix.
recordings.reviewLimit: The clip has desktop-capture segments mixed with game footage, so the narrative only claims the visible game moments.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: You re-fight after winning
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you keep playing for another fight after the first win. Fix: cash out wave, tower, reset, or end before touching another champion.
recordings.gameDetail: Honest read: this is improved, but the same leak keeps coming back after the good moment. Around 10:32 Samira is low in bot lane while Braum and Jinx can still answer, so every extra step forward is a shutdown invitation unless the next click is reset or structure. The lesson is not to fight less; it is to make the first won fight buy the wave, turret, reset, or end before accepting the second one.
recordings.eventEvidence: 1:09 Post-fight position: Samira is still forward after the fight near tower, illustrating re-engage instead of reset or structure.; 7:06 Good example: After winning, push turns to turret damage rather than chasing for another fight.; 10:32 Low health stay: Samira remains in lane near enemies instead of resetting, risking shutdown.
recordings.goodThing: The good part was real: you turned one bot-side win into turret pressure instead of only chasing kills.
recordings.whyTrust: The same game shows both sides: turret conversion is the right version, and the later low-health re-fight is how stronger opponents get shutdown gold back.
recordings.evidence: 1:09 Post-fight position: Samira is still forward after the fight near tower, illustrating re-engage instead of reset or structure.; 7:06 Good example: After winning, push turns to turret damage rather than chasing for another fight.; 10:32 Low health stay: Samira remains in lane near enemies instead of resetting, risking shutdown.
recordings.pattern: The damage is already there. The leak is the second decision: after the first win, you often accept another fight before the map payout is locked.
recordings.diamondRule: First win buys wave, tower, reset, or end before the next fight is allowed.
recordings.drill: After the first kill or forced recall, say cash out before moving forward.

At 7:58 the won lane becomes turret gold instead of more random fighting.
At 10:32 Samira is low while Braum is still present; that is a cashout moment, not a retest moment.
At 12:44 Samira is still in the extended lane fight with enemy tools active; that habit gets punished fast.
Beginner bot games reward extra fighting, so the transferable skill is conversion first, second fight only if it protects the payout.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: You stay when one hit kills you
recordings.feedbackTitle: You stay when one hit kills you
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you keep playing the wave when one auto or spell kills you. Fix: give the wave and recall before the lane turns into a death timer.
recordings.gameDetail: Honest read: this is not a mechanics issue; it is a health-gate issue. The blocker is staying in lane after the health bar says the next decision should be recall.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:13 Staying in lane at lethal health after a fight, not recalling despite danger
recordings.goodThing: After the bad early health decision, you still got back into the game and converted later pressure into the ending push.
recordings.whyTrust: This is not vague advice: the reviewed frames show the exact one-hit-health lane stay, and removing that habit protects the aggression that already works later.
recordings.evidence: Manual storyboard review of the May 18 full recording: early lane death at lethal HP, later kills and turret/base conversion after grouping.
recordings.pattern: The new game is better at converting once Samira has items, but the early lane still has a lethal-HP greed point: staying for the wave while one hit from death turns a manageable recall into a death timer.
recordings.diamondRule: Below one enemy auto or spell, the wave is no longer the objective; reset first, then play the next item spike.
recordings.drill: At low HP, say one hit kills me and recall unless the enemy bot lane is dead or fully gone.

Samira gains gold after a takedown in bot lane, with a shutdown event shown.
Samira destroys a red turret with many allied minions in bot lane.
Samira and allies destroy the final red turret in the enemy base near Nexus.

clockMoments.description: Staying in lane at lethal health after a fight, not recalling despite danger

nuance: At 2:43 the wave is not worth more than the death timer.
At 3:27 the lane is still being played from one-hit health, which better enemies punish immediately.
Later frames show better conversion: kills become turret pressure, inhibitor pressure, and nexus pressure.
The improvement is to keep the later aggression while removing the early death that delays the first real item window.
recordings.reviewLimit: Manual review used sampled replay frames and close-up storyboard sheets, not raw inputs or complete cooldown telemetry.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: 16/10 means conversion gap
recordings.feedback: The full-game read says damage is enough; the next useful rep is ending won fights through wave, tower, dragon, Baron, nexus, or recall.
recordings.gameDetail: This full review shows Samira can already create kills, so the climb gap is what happens right after the first win. The next useful rep is converting the won fight into wave, tower, objective, nexus, or recall before the next fight gives shutdown gold back.
recordings.eventEvidence: The full-game result shows high kill creation with too many deaths, which points to conversion and shutdown protection rather than raw damage.
recordings.goodThing: You are finding fights and creating damage pressure; the fix is cashing those wins out cleaner.
recordings.whyTrust: A 16/10 Samira can already create leads; reducing deaths after wins keeps shutdown gold and turns mechanics into rank pressure.
recordings.evidence: 7:22 Structure conversion: first turret destroyed after fight win; 8:19 Shutdown moment: high risk after fight win, death with gold bounty
recordings.pattern: The carry score says damage is available, so the rank leak is likely conversion after the first winning moment.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Hit the structure
recordings.feedback: At inhib or nexus, the next useful rep is ending as soon as the structure is available.
recordings.gameDetail: Once the base structure is available, kills are only a way to clear space for hitting it. The next useful rep is moving the cursor to the structure first, because structure damage is the lowest-variance conversion of the won fight.
recordings.eventEvidence: 16:12 Team focuses inhibitor during fight, prioritizing structure damage.; 16:42 Samira attacks the nexus, converting the fight win into structure pressure.
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: This is reliable because structures are guaranteed progress; extra fighting after the base is open adds variance without adding win condition.
recordings.evidence: 16:12 Team focuses inhibitor during fight, prioritizing structure damage.; 16:42 Samira attacks the nexus, converting the fight win into structure pressure.
recordings.pattern: Base fights become lower value once the structure is open.
recordings.diamondRule: Hit the structure as soon as the fight is won.
recordings.drill: After a base kill, move the cursor to the structure first.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Fight from the edge first
recordings.feedback: Let Q, autos, and W collect cooldowns before entering; Samira should clean the fight, not start it blind.
recordings.gameDetail: This clip points to Samira's best entry shape: use the edge of the fight to collect cooldown information, then dash when the reset path is real. The next useful rep is staying aggressive while delaying the full commit by one beat.
recordings.eventEvidence: 9:12 Edge entry enabled: fight unfolds with allies—perfect for collecting cooldowns, not blind dive.
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: This lowers anxiety without lowering aggression because edge play gathers real cooldown evidence before the all-in.
recordings.evidence: 9:12 Edge entry enabled: fight unfolds with allies—perfect for collecting cooldowns, not blind dive.
recordings.pattern: The edge of the fight gives Samira information without spending the dash.
recordings.diamondRule: Fight the first second from range, then enter after the enemy spends tools.
recordings.drill: Q-auto once before committing unless the target is already isolated and lethal.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Leave after the wave
recordings.feedback: Catching side farm is fine; leave toward teammates unless mid has priority or three enemies are visible.
recordings.gameDetail: Side farm is useful income until it disconnects the fed carry from the next map play. The next useful rep is taking the wave, then immediately pathing back through safe vision unless enemy positions are visible.
recordings.eventEvidence: 12:34 Nafiri near allied jungle after clearing side, not rotating toward teammates; 12:37 Nafiri starts moving toward mid as team fighting continues top, delayed join
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: This turns side farm into safe income instead of isolation, which is the difference between carrying with gold and dying with gold.
recordings.evidence: 12:34 Nafiri near allied jungle after clearing side, not rotating toward teammates; 12:37 Nafiri starts moving toward mid as team fighting continues top, delayed join
recordings.pattern: Side farm is useful until it isolates the fed carry from the next map play.
recordings.diamondRule: Catch the wave, then leave unless the enemy positions are visible.
recordings.drill: Take the wave and immediately path back through safe vision.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Name the CC before going in
recordings.feedbackTitle: Name the CC before going in
recordings.feedback: Before E/R, identify the one spell that cancels the play; enter only after it is spent, blocked by W, or aimed elsewhere.
recordings.gameDetail: This clip is about the spell that can stop Samira before the reset starts. The next useful rep is entering only after that crowd-control spell is spent, blocked, or aimed elsewhere, which keeps aggression while removing the easiest shutdown path.
recordings.eventEvidence: 4:13 Samira enters turret fight before the key CC (Swain root) is visibly used, risking interruption.; 4:22 CC threat persists; the frame shows ability effects and brawl context before the play is safe.
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: This is the highest-value check because one untracked stun, root, knock-up, or hook turns Samira's whole reset plan off.
recordings.evidence: 4:13 Samira enters turret fight before the key CC (Swain root) is visibly used, risking interruption.; 4:22 CC threat persists; the frame shows ability effects and brawl context before the play is safe.
recordings.pattern: The dangerous moment is entering before the enemy's fight-stopping spell is accounted for.
recordings.diamondRule: No full commit until the one spell that stops Samira is gone or aimed elsewhere.
recordings.drill: Name one enemy CC spell before every fight.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Count before helping
recordings.feedback: If a teammate dies nearby, count visible enemies before spending E to rescue a fight.
recordings.gameDetail: This moment is about the rescue instinct that can pull Samira into a fight already lost on numbers. The next useful rep is counting visible enemies before using E toward trouble, because skipping a bad rescue protects carry gold.
recordings.eventEvidence: 10:39 Initiation: Samira enters fight near turret, context for rescue attempt; 10:53 Death outcome: Samira is slain after engaging, shows result of not counting enemies
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: Counting visible enemies gives anxiety a concrete check; if the numbers are bad, skipping the rescue is discipline, not fear.
recordings.evidence: 10:39 Initiation: Samira enters fight near turret, context for rescue attempt; 10:53 Death outcome: Samira is slain after engaging, shows result of not counting enemies
recordings.pattern: Rescue instincts can drag Samira into a fight that is already numerically lost.
recordings.diamondRule: Count visible enemies before answering a teammate death.
recordings.drill: Say the number count before using E toward a fight.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Turn bot kills into tempo
recordings.feedback: After the first kill or forced recall, crash wave first, then choose plate, dragon move, reset, or support roam.
recordings.gameDetail: The lane win matters only if the enemy loses minions, plates, tempo, or map space after it. The next useful rep is touching the wave before looking for more fighting, because the crash makes every next option safer.
recordings.eventEvidence: 7:04 Samira focusing on minion wave after kill to crash it, setting up tempo play.; 7:00 First turret destroyed after wave crash, showing structure conversion as part of tempo advantage.
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: This is how a won lane becomes rank progress: the enemy loses minions and map space even if no second kill happens.
recordings.evidence: 7:04 Samira focusing on minion wave after kill to crash it, setting up tempo play.; 7:00 First turret destroyed after wave crash, showing structure conversion as part of tempo advantage.
recordings.pattern: Lane wins need to become minion loss, plate pressure, or objective tempo.
recordings.diamondRule: Crash before chasing the second reward.
recordings.drill: After a bot kill, touch the wave before looking for another fight.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Protect the shutdown
recordings.feedback: After a tower or multi-kill, leave with the shutdown value instead of retesting the fight.
recordings.gameDetail: Once Samira has the payout, the risk changes: another fight is no longer worth the same as the gold already held. The next useful rep is leaving with shutdown value unless the next objective is already free.
recordings.eventEvidence: 9:43 Tower destroyed—shutdown value earned, new risk context.; 9:47 Recall started—opportunity to leave safely with shutdown value.; 9:50 Samira stays—risking shutdown by not immediately leaving.
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: This is worth practicing because it does not ask for less carry pressure; it keeps the carry gold from being handed back.
recordings.evidence: 9:43 Tower destroyed—shutdown value earned, new risk context.; 9:47 Recall started—opportunity to leave safely with shutdown value.; 9:50 Samira stays—risking shutdown by not immediately leaving.
recordings.pattern: The risk shifts after Samira has gold; the next death is more expensive than the next kill is valuable.
recordings.diamondRule: Protect shutdown gold after the first payout.
recordings.drill: Recall after the payout unless an objective is already free.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Stop chasing at fog
recordings.feedback: A low target past vision is not free; shove the wave or take plate unless the next enemy position is known.
recordings.gameDetail: The low target is tempting, but fog removes the information needed to know whether the next dash is a reset or a bait. The next useful rep is stopping at vision loss and taking wave or plate unless enemy positions are known.
recordings.eventEvidence: 4:39 Low target retreats into fog with wave and plate on screen, showing stop point for the lesson.; 4:42 Both champions are low; vision loss risk as chasing starts past wave instead of shoving or plating.
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: The rule is trustworthy because fog removes the information Samira needs to decide whether the next E is a reset or a bait.
recordings.evidence: 4:39 Low target retreats into fog with wave and plate on screen, showing stop point for the lesson.; 4:42 Both champions are low; vision loss risk as chasing starts past wave instead of shoving or plating.
recordings.pattern: Chasing past fog turns a won trade into an information gamble.
recordings.diamondRule: Stop at fog unless the next enemy positions are known.
recordings.drill: When the target leaves vision, look at wave and objective before moving forward.

recordings.title: Second in at chokes
recordings.feedback: Hold the edge until enemy CC is used so Samira stays the finisher instead of the target.
recordings.gameDetail: Chokes punish the first champion in, especially when the enemy still has the spell that stops Samira. The next useful rep is being second in at jungle walls so the dash starts after the first cooldown exchange.
recordings.eventEvidence: 14:27 Samira is starting the fight at the choke with enemy CC threats still present; 14:34 Samira is killed after going in first, showing the risk of not waiting for CC to be used
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: This preserves the aggression while removing the single easiest way enemies stop Samira.
recordings.evidence: 14:27 Samira is starting the fight at the choke with enemy CC threats still present; 14:34 Samira is killed after going in first, showing the risk of not waiting for CC to be used
recordings.pattern: Chokes punish the first champion in; Samira gets more value as the second entry.
recordings.diamondRule: Second in at jungle walls unless the enemy CC is already gone.
recordings.drill: Pause at the choke edge and enter after the first cooldown exchange.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Objective before duel
recordings.feedback: When an enemy catches a wave, pressure the objective first; take the duel only with ult, summoner info, and a walk-out.
recordings.gameDetail: The side duel can look like the game, but objective pressure forces the enemy to answer on worse terms. The next useful rep is making the map ask the question first, then dueling only when ult, summoners, and exit are known.
recordings.eventEvidence: 13:41 Samira is mid lane as dragon spawns; evidence for needing objective pressure before dueling.; 13:48 Just after a duel kill and level up, showing focus was on duel, not objective.
recordings.goodThing: The useful positive sign is that Samira is creating pressure; the next step is making that pressure safer and more repeatable.
recordings.whyTrust: Objectives force the enemy to answer on your terms; random duels make the game hinge on mechanics under uncertainty.
recordings.evidence: 13:41 Samira is mid lane as dragon spawns; evidence for needing objective pressure before dueling.; 13:48 Just after a duel kill and level up, showing focus was on duel, not objective.
recordings.pattern: A side duel can distract from the objective that would make the enemy respond.
recordings.diamondRule: Objective pressure before isolated duels.
recordings.drill: Ping or path to the objective before matching the side target.

recordings.evidence: 3:34 Fight setup: allied minions push, showing potential payout windows like wave crash or plate.; 3:40 Post-commit: Samira killed, no payout secured, highlighting the need to define payout before the fight.

recordings.gameTypeSource: No queue id found in local logs
recordings.title: Make clips reviewable
recordings.feedback: Keep at least five seconds before and after the fight so the trigger, cooldowns, and exit can be judged.
recordings.gameDetail: The clip is too short to show the setup, so the review cannot honestly separate a good death, a late dash, or a missed exit. The next useful rep is evidence quality first: record enough before and after the fight so the next review can judge the decision, not just the result.
recordings.eventEvidence: The visible clip is shorter than three seconds, so the important pre-fight setup is missing.
recordings.whyTrust: The advice is low-confidence on gameplay and high-confidence on review quality because a one-second clip hides the decision that caused the outcome.
recordings.evidence: The recording is shorter than three seconds.
recordings.pattern: The clip is too short to show the decision before the result.
recordings.diamondRule: Record the trigger, the fight, and the exit so the review can judge the decision instead of the scoreboard.
recordings.drill: Capture five seconds before and after the next fight.
nuance: The visible result is not enough evidence for a confident gameplay diagnosis.
recordings.reviewLimit: Low gameplay confidence because the recording lacks pre-fight context.
